description:
Color graded with my EnduraGrade PowerGrades & LUTs: http://www.enduragrade.com

The idea of traveling solo through Patagonia always felt a little intimidating. Not because I didn’t want to go—but because I wasn’t sure how I’d handle being truly alone. No one to laugh with, no one to share the view, just me, the road, and my camera.

In this cinematic travel film, I take you with me through southern Patagonia—from the iconic granite horns of Torres del Paine National Park in Chile, to the jagged spires of Mount Fitz Roy in El Chaltén, Argentina. 

Whether you’re planning your own trip to Patagonia, dreaming of solo travel adventures, or just here for the views—I hope this story helps you feel a little more okay with not having all the answers. Thanks for being here.

--

Filmed solo on the Sony FX3. 

--

My cinema camera: https://amzn.to/4imtSC3
My travel tripod: https://amzn.to/4k49yqQ
The best tool for filming yourself: https://amzn.to/3Xszy5V
